title: Lecture 9 Sample questions without answers
body:
Lecture 9:

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Which of the following statements is false about Managed Investment Schemes (MIS), Listed Investment Companies (LICs), and Exchange-Traded Funds (ETFs) in Australia?

A. Core principles of regulations for MIS, LICs, and ETFs include minimizing conflicts of interest and clear disclosures of investment objectives and structures. B. LICs, LITs, and ETFs are similar in the sense that all have a fixed number of shares or units. C. If you invest for 1 year in a managed fund with entry and exit fees and an expense ratio, it is likely that net of fees, you would incur negative net returns even if the fund's gross return exceeded the market index by 7%. D. If a managed fund invests in unit beta stocks, the maximum beta for the fund will most likely be below 1.3.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Which of the following statements is true about Mutual Funds (MFs)?

A. A large, diversified stock mutual fund will necessarily have a beta close to 1 as large portfolios converge toward the market portfolio as the number of stocks increases. B. When evaluating the net performance of Mutual Funds’ using a one-factor or a 4-factor model is not very important as, on average, the net performance is negative regardless. C. Mutual Funds’ abnormal net performance cannot be explained by funds’ fee structure. D. Mutual fund investors are usually biased to invest in Mutual Funds with large positive previous-year historical returns.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You work as analyst for an investment bank. A client approach you to design a mutual funds solution. Her objective is to use mutual funds to create a market neutral solution exposed to momentum. For this, you identify 2 mutual funds, one of stocks and one of corporate bonds. Using historical monthly returns, the following results are obtained:

Bonds: Alpha = 0.05, Market Beta = -0.2, Momentum Beta = 0 Stocks: Alpha= -0.01, Market Beta = 0.8, Momentum Beta = 0.8

where all measures are in annual units and returns are in excess of the risk-free rate. If the market and momentum factor annual realized returns is 10% after you implement the mutual fund strategy for your client, what would be her realized annual return?  A. 5.55% B. 2.33% C. 4.25% D. 7.68% E. 5.40% F. None of the above
